steps.title: Translate "at least twice" into an inequality
steps.content: The phrase "x is at least twice the value of y" breaks into two parts: "at least" means $\geq$, and "twice the value of y" means $2y$. Together: $x \geq 2y$.

steps.title: Substitute y = 51
steps.content: Replace y with 51: $x \geq 2(51) = 102$. The boundary value is 102.

steps.title: Evaluate all choices
steps.content:
• A: $x \geq 49$ — 49 is $51 - 2$, not twice 51. Eliminated.
  • B: $x \geq 53$ — 53 is $51 + 2$, not twice 51. Eliminated.
  • C: $x \geq 102$ — $2 \times 51 = 102$. This is exactly "at least twice y." That matches Choice C — $x \geq 102$.
  • D: $x \geq 104$ — 104 is $2(51) + 2$, off by 2. Eliminated.
